 {"id":"beads_rust-8tki","title":"Conformance: Utility Commands (stats, count, stale, doctor, version, config)","description":"# Conformance: Utility Commands

## Purpose
Verify br vs bd JSON parity for utility commands: stats, count, stale, doctor, version, config.

## Current State
- stats: 1 test exists (conformance_stats)
- count: 1 test exists (conformance_count_basic)
- stale, doctor, version, config: No tests

## Test Specifications

### stats Command (5 new tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_stats_empty | Fresh workspace | ExactJson |
| conformance_stats_mixed | Open/closed mix | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_stats_with_deps | Dependency counts | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_stats_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_stats_all_fields | All stat fields present | ContainsFields |

### count Command (6 new tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_count_by_status | Group by status | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_count_by_type | Group by type | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_count_by_priority | Group by priority | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_count_by_assignee | Group by assignee | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_count_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_count_empty | No issues | ExactJson |

### stale Command (6 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_stale_default | Default threshold | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_stale_custom_days | --days 7 | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_stale_empty | No stale issues | ExactJson |
| conformance_stale_excludes_closed | Closed not stale | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_stale_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_stale_all_stale | All issues stale | NormalizedJson |

### doctor Command (5 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_doctor_healthy | Clean workspace | ExactJson |
| conformance_doctor_warnings | Warnings present | ContainsFields |
| conformance_doctor_errors | Errors present | ContainsFields |
| conformance_doctor_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_doctor_checks_all | All checks run | ContainsFields |

### version Command (4 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_version_text | Text output format | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_version_json | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_version_fields | Required fields | ContainsFields |
| conformance_version_semver | Valid semver format | ContainsFields |

### config Command (7 tests)
| Test Name | Scenario | CompareMode |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| conformance_config_list | --list shows all | NormalizedJson |
| conformance_config_get | --get specific key | ExactJson |
| conformance_config_set | --set key=value | ExitCodeOnly |
| conformance_config_get_after_set | Value persists | ExactJson |
| conformance_config_json_shape | JSON structure | StructureOnly |
| conformance_config_defaults | Default values | ContainsFields |
| conformance_config_invalid_key | Unknown key error | ExitCodeOnly |

## Logging Requirements
\`\`\`rust
// For utility commands, log key metrics
info!("conformance_{}: workspace_state={{issues:{}, closed:{}, deps:{}}}", 
    test_name, issue_count, closed_count, dep_count);
info!("conformance_{}: br_timing={:?}", test_name, br_duration);
info!("conformance_{}: bd_timing={:?}", test_name, bd_duration);
info!("conformance_{}: speedup={:.2}x", test_name, 
    bd_duration.as_millis() as f64 / br_duration.as_millis() as f64);
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 33 new conformance tests
- [ ] All utility commands have JSON parity
- [ ] Performance timing logged for all tests
- [ ] Config persistence verified across commands","notes":"Updated conformance utility-command tests in tests/conformance.rs: added log_timings helper, added --no-activity for stats, added compare_json for stats/count/stale (avg lead time excluded via FieldsExcluded), added stale_excludes_closed id check, added config_set + config_get_after_set with issue_prefix and correct br/bd args, config list/defaults now assert issue_prefix presence, doctor tests now run bd and validate checks array/name+status, version semver validates both br/bd, extract_field now supports dotted paths for nested fields. NOTE: stats comparisons exclude average_lead_time_hours due to br omitting when None. Doctor JSON schema differs between br/bd so tests validate structure per tool (exit codes + checks array), not strict JSON equality.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusNavigator","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:10:03.425687396Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T20:10:15.422638852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:56:31.173677978Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added missing tests (stats_all_fields, stale_all_stale, version_semver) and verified existing coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-8tki","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-egz8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-8tsp","title":"E2E tests: lint command","description":"# E2E Tests for `lint` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br lint` - Check for missing template sections
- `br lint --fix` - Auto-fix issues (if supported)
- `br lint --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. All issues valid → clean lint
2. Missing description → warning
3. Missing acceptance criteria → warning
4. Multiple issues with lint errors

### Error Cases
5. Lint before init → error

### Edge Cases
6. Issue with all optional fields empty
7. Issue with malformed content
8. Lint on closed issues (should skip or include?)
9. Very large workspace (1000+ issues)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_lint.rs
- [ ] 9+ test functions
- [ ] Verify lint output matches expected format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeBeaver","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:26:59.251355245Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:29:18.962698744Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T15:29:18.962698744Z","close_reason":"Added 9 lint E2E tests in tests/e2e_errors.rs per no-new-files rule; ran cargo test --test e2e_errors e2e_lint_","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-8tsp","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-914w","title":"Define DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT and MAX_LIST_LIMIT shared constants","description":"SUBTASK 4.3: The list issue default limit (50) and max limit (500) appear in two places:

Location 1 — src/mcp/tools.rs:731-732:
  let limit = raw_limit.unwrap_or(50);
  let limit = raw_limit.min(500);

Location 2 — src/cli/commands/list.rs:76:
  args.limit.unwrap_or(50);

These should be shared constants so the MCP tool and CLI command enforce the same defaults.

CHANGES:
- Define in a shared location (options: storage/mod.rs near ListFilters, or a new constants section in lib.rs):
  pub const DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT: usize = 50;
  pub const MAX_LIST_LIMIT: usize = 500;
- Update mcp/tools.rs to import and use both
- Update cli/commands/list.rs to import and use DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT

DESIGN CHOICE: These belong near the ListFilters type since they are defaults for that struct. If ListFilters is in storage/mod.rs, put them there. If thats too far from the consumers, a constants block in lib.rs works too.

WHY 50/500: 50 is a reasonable page size for both terminal display and MCP token budgets. 500 is a safety cap to prevent agents from requesting unbounded result sets that would overwhelm the MCP response.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:46.327195821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:38.087474884Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:38.087009410Z","close_reason":"DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT and MAX_LIST_LIMIT defined in mcp/tools.rs. CLI list.rs retains inline 50 with coupling comment since the MCP const is module-private and the types differ (u64 vs usize).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting"]}

These tests inflate test counts without providing regression protection.

Affected files and counts:
- src/format/rich.rs: 7 tests with zero assertions (lines 394-480)
  - test_rich_issue_table, test_rich_issue_panel, test_format_status_badge, test_format_count_badges, test_build_completion_bar, test_table_without_type_column, test_table_with_title_truncation
- src/format/theme.rs: 5 tests with zero assertions (lines 226-267)
  - test_default_theme, test_priority_styles, test_type_styles, test_dark_theme, test_minimal_theme
- src/util/progress.rs: 5 tests with zero assertions (lines 189-227)
  - test_progress_bar_hidden_when_not_terminal, test_spinner_hidden_when_not_terminal, test_progress_tracker_determinate, test_progress_tracker_spinner, test_multi_progress_hidden

Note: format/rich.rs and format/theme.rs types are dead code (superseded by output/components and output/theme — see beads_rust-5wh0). The util/progress.rs tests exercise active code but only check non-panic behavior.

Fix options:
1. Delete format/rich.rs and format/theme.rs tests along with the dead code (beads_rust-5wh0)
2. Add real assertions to util/progress.rs tests (e.g., verify bar length, verify hidden state)
3.  {"id":"beads_rust-9608","title":"Deep Code Review and Fixes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:37:36.990947095Z","updated_at":"2026-01-17T15:37:56.742083824Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T15:37:56.742044420Z","close_reason":"Completed deep review, bug fixes, and refactoring across multiple modules","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-988p","title":"[MEDIUM] README safety model overstates no-git and .beads-only guarantees","description":"Root cause: README.md uses blanket safety language that no longer matches the public CLI surface. README.md:123 says br never touches source code or runs git automatically, README.md:126 says br only touches .beads/, and README.md:585 says Never executes git. However implemented user-facing commands intentionally touch other paths or invoke git: src/cli/commands/agents.rs:951 and :1066 write AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, src/cli/commands/doctor.rs:2316 can rewrite project-root .gitignore during --repair, src/cli/commands/orphans.rs:317 and :340 run git rev-parse/log, and src/cli/commands/changelog.rs:374/:408/:422/:451 run git for explicit changelog options. Repro: inspect README lines above and rg "Command::new\("git"\)|fs::write" src/cli/commands/{orphans,changelog,agents,doctor}.rs. Proposed fix: narrow README safety promises to sync/default issue mutations, document explicit exceptions (agents, doctor --repair, changelog/orphans git inspection, config/completions/upgrade), and keep the sync-specific no-git invariant separate. Test to add: docs/CLI reference or README smoke check that safety section names exceptions when these commands exist.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:14:38.331480891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:08.866735503Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:08.866417288Z","close_reason":"Updated README and sync safety docs to scope no-git/.beads-only claims to sync/default issue storage, documented explicit git/file-writing command exceptions, and verified sync code has no runtime git subprocess calls.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":320,"issue_id":"beads_rust-988p","author":"cod_2","text":"degraded-coordination: files: README.md, docs/SYNC_SAFETY.md","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-99n","title":"Feature: ID Generation & Content Hashing","description":"# ID Generation & Content Hashing (classic)

## Purpose
Implement classic bd ID generation: **base36 adaptive length** hash IDs with collision handling, plus deterministic content hashing for dedup/export.

## ID Format
- `<prefix>-<hash>` where hash is base36 lowercase (0-9, a-z)
- Prefix from config `issue_prefix` (stored without trailing hyphen)
- Hierarchical IDs: `<parent>.<n>` (child counters table)

## Adaptive Length + Collision Handling
- Length range: 3..8 (configurable by `min_hash_length`, `max_hash_length`)
- Collision probability threshold: `max_collision_prob` (default 0.25)
- Length computed from **top-level issue count only** (exclude child IDs)
- Generation inputs: `title | description | creator | created_at (ns) | nonce`
- For each length, try nonces 0..9; if all collide, increase length

## Child IDs
- Use `child_counters` table to atomically increment next child number.
- When importing explicit child IDs, update counter to >= observed max.
- Depth limit enforced by `hierarchy.max-depth` (default 3).

## Partial ID Resolution (paired bead nz0)
- Exact match → normalized prefix → substring match on hash
- Ambiguous match returns error with candidate list

## Content Hash
- SHA256 over stable ordered fields with **null separators** between fields.
- Include: title, description, design, acceptance_criteria, notes, status, priority,
  issue_type, assignee, external_ref, **pinned**, **is_template**.
- Exclude: labels, dependencies, comments, events, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- Stored as lowercase hex string (match bd).

## Acceptance Criteria
- IDs are base36 adaptive length; collisions handled by nonce/length increase.
- Child IDs increment correctly and respect depth limit.
- Content hash deterministic and stable across runs.

## Tests
- Adaptive length thresholds with varying DB sizes.
- Collision handling (force collisions with mock).
- Child counter updates on explicit child IDs.
- Content hash stability and field inclusion/exclusion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:14:07.805886290Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:46:40.631312831Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:46:40.631312831Z","close_reason":"ID generation module complete: base36 adaptive length (3-8 chars), collision handling with nonces, content hashing (SHA256), all tests passing (48 total)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
